subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Big Bang: How It All Began
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Big Bang: How It All Began usable.

hook.text: 13.8 billion years ago, all matter, energy, space, and time erupted from a single point. That was the Big Bang.

buildup.text: It wasn't an explosion in space—it was an expansion of space itself. Every point in the universe was the center.

discovery.text: In the first second, quarks formed protons and neutrons. In three minutes, hydrogen and helium nuclei appeared.

twist.text: Nothing existed before the Big Bang—not even time. Asking 'what came before' may be a meaningless question.

climax.text: For 380,000 years, the universe was a glowing fog. Then it cooled enough for atoms to form, and light broke free.

punchline.text: No center, no edge. Space itself was born and is still expanding.

quiz.question: What does the Big Bang describe?
options: The expansion of space itself from an initial state
A massive explosion that happened somewhere in existing space
The collision of two universes that created our own
